basic_details.job_title: SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 69 VLW Posts
basic_details.department: Agriculture and Horticulture Department, Government of Sikkim
basic_details.short_information: The Sikkim Public Service Commission (SPSC) has released Advertisement No. 03/SPSC/EXAM/2026 dated 07 July 2026, inviting online applications from eligible local candidates for recruitment to 69 posts of Village Level Worker (VLW) under the Agriculture and Horticulture Department, Government of Sikkim. The SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026 drive will fill these posts on a temporary-regular basis in Level-9 of the Pay Matrix under the Sikkim State Subordinate Agriculture Service, through Direct Recruitment. Interested and eligible candidates holding a B.Sc. in Agriculture or Horticultu

application_fee.details: Candidates need to pay an application fee of ₹200/- online through Net Banking, or Credit/Debit Card (Visa/MasterCard). The fee once deposited will not be refunded or adjusted against any other purpose.

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years Maximum age: 40 years Age will be reckoned as on 31 August 2026, the closing date of application, as per Notification No. M(3)/(55)/GEN/DOP/Pt.III dated 03.07.2017.

qualification.details: Note: Candidates must fulfil all eligibility conditions, including educational qualification and age criteria, as on the closing date of application. Candidates awaiting final results are not eligible to apply, as per Notification No. 177/GEN/DOP dated 29.01.2026. | Candidates must be able to read, write, and speak at least one State language of Sikkim. Candidates must be conversant with the local customs and traditions of Sikkim. | Applications are invited only from eligible local candidates of Sikkim possessing a valid Sikkim Subject Certificate or Certificate of Identification (COI) issued by the competent authority.

vacancy_details.Category: Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability, etc.

pwd_wise_breakdown.category: Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability, etc.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be placed in Level-9 of the Pay Matrix under the Sikkim State Subordinate Agriculture Service. During the period of Probation/Apprenticeship/Training, pay shall be governed as per Notification No. 489/GEN/DOP dated 31.10.2011.

selection_process: The selection of candidates for the SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026 will be based on the following stages:

Written Examination — as per the scheme and pattern given in Appendix-I of the official notification. Document Scrutiny — candidates who qualify in the written examination will be called for verification of original/attested documents on dates specified by the Commission.
Candidates are advised to download the detailed syllabus and exam pattern from the official SPSC website before beginning preparation.
how_to_apply: Visit the official SPSC website: spsc.sikkim.gov.in
Go through the detailed advertisement and read all instructions carefully before proceeding.
Register/login and fill in the online application form for Advertisement No. 03/SPSC/EXAM/2026.
Upload the required documents and photograph/signature as specified.
Pay the application fee of ₹200/- online via Net Banking or Credit/Debit Card.
Submit the application on or before 31 August 2026 and save a copy of the confirmation for future reference.

Note: Applications received through any mode other than online will not be accepted and will be summarily rejected. Applications submitted after the stipulated date and time will not be accepted, and SPSC will not be responsible for the same.
important_instructions: No TA/DA is admissible for attending the examination. Admission to all stages of the examination is purely provisional and subject to satisfying the prescribed eligibility conditions. If, at any stage of verification, a candidate is found ineligible, their candidature will be cancelled by the Commission without notice. The Commission will entertain applications for review or RTI/correspondence only after the entire recruitment process is complete. Any further instructions, corrigendum, or addendum will be uploaded only on the official SPSC website. E-Admit cards can be downloaded after a notice is published by the Commission on its official website. Candidates must carry a valid Photo ID proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter's Card, or Driving License) along with the e-admit card to the examination centre.

rows.event: Last Date for Submission of Online Application

content_sections.heading: SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026: Category-Wise Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The 69 Village Level Worker posts are distributed across various reservation categories as notified by the Commission. Candidates should check their applicable category carefully before applying. Note: If sufficient eligible women candidates are not available up to the 33% earmarked quota, unfilled vacancies shall be filled by male candidates from the same reserved category, as per Notification No. M(14)/136/GEN/DOP dated 26.11.2018. There is no carry forward of roster points reserved for SPAE, ESM and PFAR categories. If eligible candidates are not available even after interexchange among BL/ST/OBC(CL)/OBC(SL)/SC/PT/MBC(SL)/WSS categories, unfilled vacancies shall be filled by candidates from the same reserved category as per Notification No. M(14)/136/GEN/DOP dated 26.11.2018.

tables.caption: Reservation for PWD, Sports Persons/Artisans of Excellence, Ex-Servicemen and Paramilitary Forces

rows.category: Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability, etc.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Note: Candidates must fulfil all eligibility conditions, including educational qualification and age criteria, as on the closing date of application. Candidates awaiting final results are not eligible to apply, as per Notification No. 177/GEN/DOP dated 29.01.2026. Candidates must be able to read, write, and speak at least one State language of Sikkim. Candidates must be conversant with the local customs and traditions of Sikkim. Applications are invited only from eligible local candidates of Sikkim possessing a valid Sikkim Subject Certificate or Certificate of Identification (COI) issued by the competent authority.

rows.qualification_required: B.Sc. in Agriculture or Horticulture from an ICAR-recognized University or Institute. Candidates holding an M.Sc. in any discipline of Agriculture or Horticulture must mandatorily also possess a B.Sc. in Agriculture or Horticulture.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Minimum age: 18 years Maximum age: 40 years Age will be reckoned as on 31 August 2026, the closing date of application, as per Notification No. M(3)/(55)/GEN/DOP/Pt.III dated 03.07.2017.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Sikkim Subject Certificate or Certificate of Identification (COI) issued by the competent authority Category Certificate issued by the competent authority of the Government of Sikkim Valid Local Employment Card issued by the appropriate authority No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Head of Department, in case of an employed candidate Marital Status Certificate for female candidates; married women candidates must enclose COI/Sikkim Subject Certificate of their husband

content_sections.heading: SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Candidates need to pay an application fee of ₹200/- online through Net Banking, or Credit/Debit Card (Visa/MasterCard). The fee once deposited will not be refunded or adjusted against any other purpose.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection of candidates for the SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026 will be based on the following stages: Written Examination — as per the scheme and pattern given in Appendix-I of the official notification. Document Scrutiny — candidates who qualify in the written examination will be called for verification of original/attested documents on dates specified by the Commission. Candidates are advised to download the detailed syllabus and exam pattern from the official SPSC website before beginning preparation.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Note: Applications received through any mode other than online will not be accepted and will be summarily rejected. Applications submitted after the stipulated date and time will not be accepted, and SPSC will not be responsible for the same.

content_sections.heading: Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be placed in Level-9 of the Pay Matrix under the Sikkim State Subordinate Agriculture Service. During the period of Probation/Apprenticeship/Training, pay shall be governed as per Notification No. 489/GEN/DOP dated 31.10.2011.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: No TA/DA is admissible for attending the examination. Admission to all stages of the examination is purely provisional and subject to satisfying the prescribed eligibility conditions. If, at any stage of verification, a candidate is found ineligible, their candidature will be cancelled by the Commission without notice. The Commission will entertain applications for review or RTI/correspondence only after the entire recruitment process is complete. Any further instructions, corrigendum, or addendum will be uploaded only on the official SPSC website. E-Admit cards can be downloaded after a notice is published by the Commission on its official website. Candidates must carry a valid Photo ID proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter's Card, or Driving License) along with the e-admit card to the examination centre.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Sikkim Public Service Commission (SPSC) has announced a total of 69 Village Level Worker (VLW) vacancies under the Agriculture and Horticulture Department, Government of Sikkim.

faq.question: Q2. What is the post name under SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The post name is Village Level Worker (VLW) under the Sikkim State Subordinate Agriculture Service.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a B.Sc. in Agriculture or Horticulture from an ICAR-recognized University or Institute. Candidates with an M.Sc. in Agriculture/Horticulture must also possess a B.Sc. in the same field.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit is 18 years and the maximum age limit is 40 years. The age will be calculated as on 31 August 2026.

faq.question: Q5. Who can apply for SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only eligible local candidates of Sikkim having a valid Sikkim Subject Certificate or Certificate of Identification (COI) issued by the competent authority can apply.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹200/-. Candidates can pay the fee online through Net Banking or Credit/Debit Card.

faq.question: Q7. What is the last date to apply online for SPSC Village Level Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form is 31 August 2026.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for selected candidates in SPSC VLW Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive salary under Level-9 of the Pay Matrix of the Sikkim State Subordinate Agriculture Service.
